ddls:
CREATE TABLE "processors" (
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processor */,
  "processor_name" TEXT /* Name of the processor */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the processor's features */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manufacturer of the processor */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the processor was released */,
  "end_of_life" TEXT /* Date when the processor will no longer be supported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated processor */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the feature */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the feature */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the feature */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the feature is critical for system operation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_features_processor_id FOREIGN KEY ("processor_id") REFERENCES processors ("processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated feature */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "allowable_values" TEXT /* Allowed values for the parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter */,
  "vhdl_type" TEXT /* Data type used in VHDL */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum allowable value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum allowable value for the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "is_configurable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter can be configured by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_feature_id FOREIGN KEY ("feature_id") REFERENCES features ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each value entry */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Actual value assigned to the parameter */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the configuration */,
  "set_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who set the value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameter_values_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameter_values_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated processor */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version changes */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who approved the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_processor_id FOREIGN KEY ("processor_id") REFERENCES processors ("processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version affected by the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Additional comments about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the log event */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log event */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert condition */,
  "alert_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert occurred */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string representing the permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
